paper_title: Analytical and Provenance Analysis of Marine Sediments

methods.description: Process for identifying and analyzing clay minerals and geochemical properties of marine sediments using X-ray diffraction and X-ray fluorescence.

steps.description: Clay minerals were identified by X-ray diffraction (XRD) using a PANalytical X'Pert PRO diffractometer on oriented mounts of noncalcareous, clay-sized particles.
steps.searchable_summary: identify clay minerals using XRD

steps.description: Pretreatment of oriented mounts and measurements were based on the method described by Liu et al. (2004).
steps.searchable_summary: pre-treat clay samples for XRD analysis

steps.description: After the removal of carbonate and organic matter, the clay minerals (<2 μm) were separated using the principle of Stokes' settling velocity.
steps.searchable_summary: separate clay minerals using Stokes' law

steps.description: The extracted clay minerals were smeared on the surface of the glass slides, and they were then dried at room temperature.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare clay slides for XRD

steps.description: Three XRD runs were performed under air-dried, ethylene-glycol solvation for 24 hr, and heating at 490°C for 2 hr.
steps.searchable_summary: perform XRD analysis under different conditions

steps.description: Identification and interpretation of the clay minerals were made mainly according to the (001) basal reflections on the three XRD diagrams.
steps.searchable_summary: interpret XRD diagrams for clay mineral identification

steps.description: Semiquantitative estimates of peak areas of the basal reflections for smectite, illite, and kaolinite/chlorite were carried out on the glycolated curve by using the MacDiff software.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate peak areas using MacDiff software

steps.description: Relative proportions of kaolinite and chlorite were separated by relative proportions according to the 3.57/3.54 Å peak areas.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate relative proportions of kaolinite and chlorite

steps.description: Some mineralogical characters of illite were determined on the glycolated curve.
steps.searchable_summary: determine illite characteristics from XRD

steps.description: The illite crystallinity was expressed as the full width at half maximum of the illite 10 Å peak.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate illite crystallinity

steps.description: The illite chemical index was estimated from the 5/10 Å illite peak areas.
steps.searchable_summary: estimate illite chemical index

steps.description: Analysis of major elements was performed on bulk sediments by X-ray fluorescence, using a PANalytical Axios MAX Spectrometer.
steps.searchable_summary: analyze major elements using XRF

steps.description: The dried sediments were ground to powder with an agate pestle and mortar.
steps.searchable_summary: grind sediments to powder

steps.description: About 4 g of the powder was mixed with H3BO3 in a set of cylinder mold, and then compressed with 200 kN.
steps.searchable_summary: prepare sediment pellets for XRF

steps.description: Overall analytical precision and accuracy were monitored by measurements of the national geostandards GSR-6 and GSD-15.
steps.searchable_summary: validate analysis with geostandards

methods.description: Procedure for dating marine sediment samples using AMS 14C and establishing an age model with Bayesian statistics.

steps.description: For four samples on the upper part of the core with relatively high carbonate contents, more than 8 mg of well-preserved planktonic Globigerinoides ruber shells were picked from each sample for AMS 14C dating.
steps.searchable_summary: select planktonic shells for AMS 14C dating

steps.description: For samples with low carbonate contents, more than 8 mg of mixed species of planktonic and benthic foraminifera were picked for AMS 14C dating.
steps.searchable_summary: select mixed foraminifera for AMS 14C dating

steps.description: The AMS 14C dating was done at Beta Analytic Laboratory, USA.
steps.searchable_summary: perform AMS 14C dating at laboratory

steps.description: The carbonate content was determined by a reaction with HCl in a pressure calcimeter that had been calibrated.
steps.searchable_summary: determine carbonate content using pressure calcimeter

steps.description: One in every 10 samples was determined in replication for quality control.
steps.searchable_summary: perform quality control on carbonate content

steps.description: The age depth relationship was calculated with the Bayesian statistic by using the Bacon age-modeling.
steps.searchable_summary: calculate age-depth relationship using Bayesian statistics

methods.description: Analysis of the source and transport processes of terrigenous sediments using clay mineral assemblages and geochemical indices.

steps.description: Clay mineral assemblages of Core SO18383-3 and river samples of potential sources are plotted in a ternary diagram.
steps.searchable_summary: plot clay mineral assemblages in ternary diagram

steps.description: Illite chemistry index together with illite crystallinity are plotted to trace a significant source of illite.
steps.searchable_summary: plot illite chemistry and crystallinity
